primary_variables.matched_definition: Comparison between disease and matched control cohorts.
evidence: Maternal serum levels of calcyclin and heat shock protein 90 are compared throughout pregnancy from the first trimester till term among women with preeclampsia (n = 43) and age‐matched normotensive pregnant controls (n = 46).
A case‐control study was conducted at the Department of Obstetrics and Gynecology of the Erasmus MC, Rotterdam, the Netherlands.

replicate_hints: three technical (three PRM measurements of an identical sample (quality control) throughout the assay run) and three methodological (three independently prepared replicates of an SCX‐fractionated serum sample) replicates were measured

evidence: Serum levels of calcyclin are significantly lower in patients with preeclampsia in the second trimester of pregnancy as compared to controls (p < 0.05).
Serum levels of heat shock protein 90 are significantly higher in patients with preeclampsia in the third trimester as compared to controls (p < 0.001).

Maternal serum levels of calcyclin and heat shock protein 90 are compared throughout pregnancy from the first trimester till term among women with preeclampsia (n = 43) and age‐matched normotensive pregnant controls (n = 46).

base_candidates_ranked.reason: The experiment uses Parallel Reaction Monitoring (PRM), which is a targeted acquisition method. While not DDA, it is the closest base for targeted quantification if a specific PRM workflow is not available, but it requires a targeted search approach.

modifier_candidates_ranked.reason: Explicitly stated multiple times that a 'serum-based 2D LC-MS assay using Parallel Reaction Monitoring (PRM)' was used.
Used stable isotope-labeled (SIL) peptides as internal standards for quantification (LQDAEIAR and YIDQEELNK).
